name: Ohio State University Extension - Butler County
description_short: Ohio State University Extension's Butler County office provides research-based educational programs on healthy finances, nutrition, food safety, and family relationships at no cost to residents.
description_long: Ohio State University Extension has served Ohio communities since 1914, and the Butler County office operates as part of the College of Food, Agricultural, and Environmental Sciences. The Family and Consumer Sciences (FCS) program is their primary consumer finance resource, delivering evidence-based educational content designed to help residents make informed financial decisions. The program emphasizes accessibility and impartiality, translating university research into practical guidance for all Ohioans regardless of income or background. The Butler County office is staffed by Heather Reister, the Family & Consumer Sciences Educator, who can be reached at 513-887-3722 or via email. Their mission centers on empowering people to adopt practices that improve financial health, strengthen relationships, and promote nutritious lifestyles. The organization positions itself as a trusted, non-commercial source of financial and life skills education. Unlike commercial services, there are no fees, sales pitches, or profit motives—the focus is purely on community education and skill-building. This makes them a legitimate free-help resource aligned with similar NFCC-certified counseling organizations. A key strength is the program's grounding in peer-reviewed research and university credibility, ensuring information currency and accuracy. The main caveat is that this is an educational program, not a crisis intervention or one-on-one counseling service. While they offer financial literacy content, they do not provide personalized debt counseling, credit repair, or case management typical of HUD-approved housing counselors or credit counseling agencies.
pros: Completely free educational programs with no fees or commercial agenda
Research-based content developed by university professionals and faculty
Accessible to all Ohioans through county extension network since 1914
Covers multiple life domains (finances, nutrition, relationships, food safety) in integrated approach
Direct contact with local educator (Heather Reister) for program information and guidance
Monthly parenting tips email sign-up available for ongoing education
Part of established university system with credibility and institutional backing
cons: Educational program only—does not provide personalized one-on-one financial counseling or case management
No credit repair, debt settlement, or dispute services available
Limited information on website about specific financial literacy curriculum, workshops, or scheduling
Generic contact method (educator email/phone) with no indication of immediate availability or response times
No clear indication whether programs address credit-building, emergency assistance, or debt-specific topics
best_for: Families and individuals seeking free, research-based financial education and money management skills
Parents looking for parenting tips, family relationship guidance, and healthy living information
Ohioans interested in food safety, nutrition education, and healthy aging resources
Community members wanting trustworthy, non-commercial educational resources
diagnosis: Best for Ohioans seeking free, research-backed financial and life skills education without commercial pressure. Primary caveat: this is an educational program, not a personalized financial counseling, debt management, or credit repair service—individuals needing one-on-one crisis intervention or specialized debt solutions should seek NFCC-certified counseling agencies.
services: Family and Consumer Sciences (FCS) educational programming
Expanded Food and Nutrition Education Program (EFNEP)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
